action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 26's explore.py crashed on enhance_batch without lengths, but confirmed the baseline PESQ on a subset. All training/adapter attempts have failed. Post-processing (alpha, double-pass, RMS, high-pass) hasn't improved test PESQ over the 3.134 baseline. Analysis: A critical untested discrepancy is that SpeechBrain's Pretrained inference classes typically include an audio_normalizer that scales inputs before neural processing and rescales outputs afterward. Our manual enhance_batch path in predict.py completely bypasses this normalization. If the pretrained MetricGAN+ expects normalized inputs (e.g., peak-normalized to [-1,1] with a specific scale), skipping normalization could degrade PESQ. Native enhance_file should handle this automatically. Decision: Write a validation script that compares our manual batch path against SpeechBrain's native enhance_file (which includes normalization/denormalization), and also tests manually applying the model's audio_normalizer before enhance_batch. If either improves validation PESQ, update predict.py immediately.
